title: start podman container via jenkins; some process are gone after run completes
body:
Hi; I have a container that is started using podman; via Jenkins. I am finding that after the Jenkins job completes; the container is still in running state (as expected) but some of it processes including network interface slirp4netns is nolonger running. I am nolonger able to do such things as exec to the container or ssh to it. Note that running this scenario outside  jenkins in the nodes terminal; the container stays accessible if logout and log back in. So I am thinking it is something related to how jenkins is starting the container from the user  session. I have Jenkins setup to launch the agent via ssh using same user I access via node.

I am running in detached mod and  have tried to run the container in privileged mode and with user session enable-linger ; but this did not help. Thanks in advance,



```
$ podman --version
podman version 4.0.2
```

**Output of `podman info`:**


```
    podman run --name $IMAGE_NAME                \
               --hostname $CM_HOST               \
               --systemd=true                    \
               --privileged                      \
                        -p 7180:7180             \
                        -p few other ports ..           \
            -id $FINAL_IMAGE

```

Nolonger able to ssh
```
$ ssh cms.dv.net hostname
ssh: connect to host localhost port 49122: Cannot assign requested address

$ podman exec -it $(podman ps -qa) bash
Error: OCI runtime error: runc: exec failed: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: transport endpoint is not connected

```

```
**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.0.2-6.module+el8.6.0+14877+f643d2d6.x86_64

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Is there a potential that Jenkins is closing the processes in question itself?

> Is there a potential that Jenkins is closing the processes in question itself?

Not sure  why jenkins would close 1 process but not others! Could we be losing the parent process? 
Show user shows `Linger=yes`; what else would I need to set to keep the network  slirp4netns from going down?
```
$ loginctl show-user salimz
UID=26769
GID=7777
Name=salimz
Timestamp=Tue 2022-08-16 09:46:20 PDT
TimestampMonotonic=3377986
RuntimePath=/run/user/26769
Service=user@26769.service
Slice=user-26769.slice
Display=2
State=active
Sessions=725 724 721 478 376 375 373 204 203 5 3 2
IdleHint=no
IdleSinceHint=0
IdleSinceHintMonotonic=0
Linger=yes
```
These processes are managed by Conmon. I don't see Conmon in either of your `lsns` outputs (though we don't expect it to have namespaces, so we don't expect it to be there). Is Conmon still running?

No; I don't see Common running
```
$ ps -ef | grep -i common
salimz   1503797 1388840  0 08:45 pts/0    00:00:00 grep --color=auto -i common

```

it might be that slirp4netns is not moved to a separate cgroup, so it is terminated when the current cgroup is terminated.

ok, finally got Jenkins to not kill processes spawned by a job.

Jenkins uses a special mechanism to cleanup child processes. It looks for process with BUILD_ID matching the build number (In case of Jenkins Pipeline use JENKINS_NODE_COOKIE instead of BUILD_ID). You can set that to different value and jenkins will skip killing the process.

Here is whataI endup doing:
```
    JENKINS_NODE_COOKIE=dontKillMe nohup ./script.sh --run &
    pid=$!
    wait $pid
```
see the below links for moreinfo.
- https://stackoverflow.com/questions/39169457/how-to-tell-jenkins-not-to-kill-processes-after-successful-execution-of-job-in-m
- https://wiki.jenkins-ci.org/display/JENKINS/ProcessTreeKiller
